question: In the situation shown in Figure, when vehicle A passes the point indicated by X, it must slow down.
description: Vehicle A doesn't have to slow down because it has the right of way.

question: When driving down a long downward slope, drivers of four-wheeled vehicles should mainly apply the foot brake and drivers of two-wheeled vehicles should mainly apply the brakes on the front and back wheels. Engine braking should be used as an auxiliary.
description: Drivers should use mainly engine braking and apply the foot brake as an auxiliary.

question: When parking or stopping a regular motor vehicle on a general road at night, even if the driver positions an emergency warning reflector device at the rear of the vehicle, he or she must turn on the hazard lights, parking lights, or tail lamps.
description: The hazard lights, parking lights, or tail lamps are not necessary when an emergency reflector device is positioned.

question: The owner of an automobile (excluding two-wheeled motor vehicles) must obtain a personal parking place for it that is not on a road and is within 5 kilometers of the address or other base position from which the automobile will be used.
description: The personal parking place must be within 2 km.

question: Parking and stopping are permitted in tunnels with vehicular lanes.
description: Neither parking nor stopping a vehicle is allowed in tunnels with and without vehicular lanes.

question: Where there is a traffic sign as shown in Figure, vehicles must not change lanes or pass on the side in order to overtake automobiles or general mopeds.
description: In a no overtaking zone, vehicles must not change lanes in order to overtake automobiles or general mopeds.

question: The method of passing around the curve as shown in Figure is correct.
description: Drivers should slow down before the curve and accelerate at the end of the curve.

question: When a vehicle attempts to overtake the vehicle ahead, the vehicle ahead may not notice and begin to change to the right lane. If the driver of the overtaking vehicle senses danger, he or she may sound the horn.
description: The horn can be used when needed to avoid danger.

question: Parking is prohibited within 5 meters of a fire hydrant, a place where firefighting equipment is stored, or a fire alarm.
description: Parking is prohibited within 1 meter of a fire alarm.

question: Vehicles other than large-size trucks loaded with cargo may not use the slower traffic lane of an expressway.
description: Any vehicles that cannot satisfy the minimum speed limit are allowed to use it.

question: Where there is a traffic sign as shown in Figure, vehicles loaded with cargo of a height of up to 3.3 meters from the cargo bed may proceed.
description: Only vehicles with a total aboveground height of up to 3.3 meters may proceed.

question: When there are passengers getting on and off a streetcar, a driver must stop until all of the passengers or other people finish crossing the road, regardless of whether there is a safety zone or not.
description: Vehicles may proceed at a low speed when passing a safety zone.

question: The traffic sign shown in Figure designates a national expressway or a motorway.
description: This is a sign designating motor vehicles only, and is placed on a national expressway or a motorway.

question: An automobile may stop for ten minutes on a road where there is less than 3.5 meters of space between the right side of the vehicle and the right side of the road while the driver stands beside the vehicle and gives instructions for the loading of cargo.
description: Drivers are allowed to park when loading or unloading cargo and the driver is immediately available to drive.

question: When tires wear down, the frictional resistance between the road surface and the tires decreases. Braking distance increases but reaction distance is not affected.
description: The reaction distance has nothing to do with worn tires because the brakes don't start to operate at the reaction distance.

question: When a vehicle collides with an object at rest, if it can decrease its speed to half, the kinetic energy at the time of impact will be just about one-quarter of that generated when speed is not deceased.
description: The kinetic energy at the time of a collision is proportional to the square of speed. 1/2 x 1/2 = 1/4.

question: The use of a child seat is not required for a preschool child less than six years of age riding in the rear seat of a four-wheeled vehicle.
description: The use of a child seat is required on the rear seat as well.

question: If loading cargo that cannot be divided as shown in Figure, a driver need not obtain a permit from the police chief having jurisdiction over the place of departure.
description: A permit must be obtained because the total length extends beyond more than 1/10th of the length behind the automobile.

question: The hydroplaning phenomenon, which occurs while driving at high speed, is the 'standing wave' phenomenon that occurs when tire pressure is low.
description: Wave shaped ripples on the tire are called the standing wave phenomenon.

question: The seating capacity noted on the motor vehicle inspection certificate does not include the driver.
description: The driver is included in the seating capacity.

question: When the hand brake of a four-wheeled vehicle is pulled all the way, there should not be any room to pull it further.
description: There should be room to pull it further. Otherwise the brake doesn't operate well.

question: Centrifugal force increases proportionally to the square of speed, and also increases as the radius of the curve decreases.
description: This is a law of nature (law of physics). One must be fully aware of it when driving on a curved road.

question: When there is enough passing clearance on the left side of the vehicle ahead, a vehicle may overtake the vehicle ahead by passing on the left side.
description: When overtaking, a vehicle, in principle, must pass on the right side of the vehicle overtaken.

question: If a vehicle’s center of gravity becomes high and it leans to one side it is likely to turn over, so cargo must be loaded as low as possible and distributed to the left and right rather than on one side.
description: The vehicle would not be stable unless the center of gravity is low and evenly balanced in the middle of the vehicle.

question: When towing a broken-down vehicle using a towing rope, the distance between vehicles must be 5 meters or less and a red cloth with an area of 0.3 square meters or more must be attached to the center of the rope.
description: Attach a white cloth not a red cloth.

question: When an oncoming vehicle is crossing the centerline to overtake another vehicle, a driver has no choice except to cut in front of a vehicle that is displaying a novice driver sign.
description: Such an action cannot be helped to avoid a head-on collision.

question: Correct driving posture enables reliable driving performance and reduces fatigue, so drivers should not relax their driving posture by tilting their body or driving with their elbow resting on the window ledge.
description: It is important to maintain a posture that allows the driver to correctly operate the steering wheel or pedals in case of an emergency.
